refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. The refinement clarifies the requirement by specifying the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring the FR remains solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design system components with redundancy to ensure continuous operation despite failures
Implement real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance to minimize downtime
Establish robust operational procedures and safety plans to prevent operational interruptions
Incorporate modular design to allow quick replacement or repair of faulty components
Use fault-tolerant control algorithms to maintain operation under degraded conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design system components with redundancy to ensure continuous operation despite failures

potential_sources: Redundancy design inherently supports independence by isolating failures
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy as a DP supports independence by allowing one component to fail without affecting overall availability. This reduces coupling between components and aligns well with the FR of achieving high operational availability.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy may increase system weight or power consumption
Assumes operational policies support maintenance of redundant components
Risk that monitoring and maintenance systems may not detect all failure modes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance strategies as complementary DPs to support availability and reduce risks associated with redundancy alone.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system reliability and continuous operation through redundancy and failure management

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts about system failures and redundancy status to other modules

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives and sends system health and status data for monitoring redundancy effectiveness

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates redundancy status and failure information across networked components

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system-level module; contains redundancy concept but diffusion is high due to many unrelated functions.
Physical vehicle module; redundancy design is relevant but mostly implemented in subsystems, causing high diffusion.
Focused on remote control; redundancy design is peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Core responsibility for system safety and continuous operation; best containment with minimal diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The redundancy design DP is best contained within the Safety Services module, as it directly aligns with the module's responsibility for ensuring continuous operation and managing failures. This module can encapsulate redundancy mechanisms effectively, minimizing diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical redundancy mechanisms are tightly coupled to hardware.
Dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce coupling complexity.
If Safety Services module grows too large, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks materialize, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate physical redundancy (Vehicle) from logical redundancy management (Safety Services) to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP to design system components with redundancy directly addresses the FR to achieve operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target. The DP logically satisfies the functional requirement by focusing on continuous operation despite failures, which aligns with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical redundancy mechanisms are tightly coupled to hardware.
Dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce coupling complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows the highest containment score (9) and lowest diffusion score (2), indicating it is the best architectural boundary for the redundancy DP. This module's scope aligns well with managing redundancy and failure, minimizing diffusion risk compared to other modules with broader or less relevant responsibilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring system availability metrics (≥ 0.97) over the evaluation period, supported by data collected via the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces managed by the Safety Services module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the redundancy DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces for health monitoring and failure alerts, enabling effective realization of the FR within a defined module boundary.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the potential overlap between physical redundancy in the Vehicle module and logical redundancy management in Safety Services; if complexity grows, consider splitting the DP into physical and logical redundancy components allocated to Vehicle and Safety Services respectively.
Manage dependencies on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces carefully to avoid coupling complexity that could increase diffusion risk.

Ensure the Safety Services module does not become a bottleneck or single point of failure by possibly distributing redundancy management functions or implementing failover strategies within the module.

decision.justification: Although the current DP and IM pass the gates and align well with the FR, the high safety criticality and identified risks such as potential overlap between physical and logical redundancy, coupling complexity via Telemetry and Network Link interfaces, and the risk of the Safety Services module becoming a bottleneck or single point of failure warrant further decomposition. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will enable clearer separation of physical and logical redundancy concerns, better management of dependencies, and distribution of redundancy management functions to mitigate risks and ensure robust safety-critical design.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure physical redundancy mechanisms maintain continuous operation despite hardware failures.
Manage logical redundancy to monitor and control system availability and failure recovery.
Isolate and manage dependencies on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to minimize coupling complexity.
Distribute redundancy management functions within Safety Services to prevent bottlenecks and single points of failure.

revise.why: Refine the FR to explicitly separate physical and logical redundancy aspects and include requirements for managing dependencies and distribution of redundancy management to address identified risks.

assumptions: The Safety Services module can be architected to distribute redundancy management functions effectively.
Telemetry and Network Link interfaces can be modularized to reduce coupling complexity.
open_risks: Potential overlap and complexity between physical and logical redundancy implementations.
Dependency-induced coupling via Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
Safety Services module becoming a single point of failure or bottleneck.
evidence_needed: Detailed analysis of physical vs logical redundancy separation feasibility.
Interface design documentation for Telemetry and Network Link modules.
Architectural plans for distributing redundancy management within Safety Services.
